capability_objective: The events lead is checking lodging holds referenced in two venue notes. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the reservation named “Northstar Speaker Lodging” at “Minneapolis” and the reservation named “Lakeside Volunteer Rooms” at “Madison”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use the headings “First record” and “Second record” in the requested order, then one unheaded sentence comparing the statuses; omit unrelated matches.
prompt:
The events lead is checking two lodging holds referenced in venue notes. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `lodging-audit.md` from full reservation records, not search summaries or backing files. Use the genuine local `./lodgectl` executable through Pi's shell tool. Read `LODGING.md` and use the executable's built-in help to discover its interface. Do not inspect the protected seed, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier, and do not write a script or wrapper to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the reservation named “Northstar Speaker Lodging” at “Minneapolis” and the reservation named “Lakeside Volunteer Rooms” at “Madison”. Your first lodging-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./lodgectl` processes, with no other lodging operation in that action. Wait for both results. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following lodging-data assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./lodgectl` processes, using only the IDs returned by their respective searches. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Omit unrelated matches. Take status values only from successful full-record gets.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the lodging data. Do not expose stable IDs, unrelated records, or extra full-record fields in the deliverable.

`lodging-audit.md` must contain exactly two level-two Markdown sections in the requested order, followed by exactly one unheaded comparison sentence. Use these exact shapes, replacing placeholders:
`## First record`
`Northstar Speaker Lodging (Minneapolis): <first-status-or-unresolved>`

`## Second record`
`Lakeside Volunteer Rooms (Madison): <second-status-or-unresolved>`

`Northstar Speaker Lodging is <first-status-or-unresolved> and Lakeside Volunteer Rooms is <second-status-or-unresolved>, so the statuses <match|differ|cannot be compared>.`

Use `match` or `differ` only when both statuses came from successful full-record gets; otherwise use `cannot be compared`. Use the literal lowercase word `unresolved` for an unresolved branch, preserve retrieved status spelling and punctuation, and include no other content. Return only the same sections and sentence in your final response. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the protected environment and verifier files unchanged.
